theme: 9:30 candle followthrough — when the predicted side gets retested, does the day close in that direction?
builds_on: analyses/extended-stats/04-conditional-on-color (which established that green 9:30 -> 74% high retest, red 9:30 -> 74% low retest)

table_1_color_x_retest_x_dayclose.description: Given the 9:30 candle's color AND which side got retested by 10:00, does today close in the predicted direction (green day after green 9:30, red day after red 9:30)?

rows.reading: Strong continuation — when the high gets retested on a green-9:30 day, today closes green ~72% of the time.
When the wrong side retests (low on a green day), today is essentially a coin flip.
Mirror of green case — strong continuation. Red-9:30 + low retest -> today closes red ~70%.

table_2_body_size_split.description: Same setups split by 9:30 body size: 'wide body' = body is >=50% of the candle's range; 'narrow body' = <50%.

rows.reading: Wide-body green 9:30 + high retest is the strongest bullish setup we've measured.
Still bullish but ~10pp weaker than wide-body version.
Strongest bearish setup. Wide-body red + low retest -> today closes red ~80%.
Big drop-off. Narrow-body red is much weaker than wide-body version.

table_3_excursion_past_high.description: On Green 9:30 + high retest days only: how far does price travel past the 9:30 high during the rest of the session, expressed as % of the 9:30 candle's range?
table_3_excursion_past_high.interpretation: Strong followthrough. ~81% of green-9:30+hi-retest days extend MORE than 50% of the 9:30 range past the high. Mean follow-through is 1.21x the 9:30 range. About 17.6% of days, price extends more than 2x the 9:30 range past the high — large directional moves are common.

key_findings.1: The 'wrong side' retest (green 9:30 + low retest, or red 9:30 + high retest) destroys the directional edge from extended-stats #4. Color predicts WHICH side gets retested first, but the day's close direction depends on which side ACTUALLY gets retested.
key_findings.2: Wide-body 9:30 candles produce ~10pp stronger continuation than narrow-body. Red-side asymmetry is bigger (80% vs 55%) than green-side (77% vs 67%).
key_findings.3: When the directional setup works, follow-through is large. Mean excursion past the 9:30 high on green+hiret days is ~1.2x the 9:30 range itself. Almost half (49.4%) of green+hiret days extend more than 1x the range past the high.

sample_size_caveat: 232 days for the base sample. Sub-buckets (n=25-49) have wider confidence intervals (~±10pp). The 71% / 70% headline rates are reliable to ~±5pp. Body-size sub-splits should be treated as directional, not precise.
